6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 description:
That envelope reached the right hands.
Frightened men remember what they owe. One of Prapor's people suddenly remembered an old Reserve parts cache that never made it onto the books. RB-ST. AK furniture, worn spares, magazines, cheap army scraps — not glamorous, but every crew starts with leftovers before it owns warehouses.
Get through the lock, find the cache, mark it, and leave. Do not drag metal across the base like a starving scav. My crew will collect what Prapor forgot, and the first rule gets written: what falls out of their books lands on my table.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 note: Tony wants you to access RB-ST on Reserve and mark a forgotten army parts cache for his crew.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 startedMessageT: Reserve. RB-ST. Find the old army parts cache and mark it. Do not get greedy. This is route work, not shopping.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 successMessageT: Good. The cache is marked. Prapor's man gave us the memory, you opened the door, and now those leftovers move under my name.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 failMessageText: You missed the lock, missed the cache, or made noise where I needed a mark. Useful things disappear when men get sloppy.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 acceptPlayerMes: I'll get into RB-ST and mark the cache.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the RB-ST cache marked for his crew, not looted.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba923: Locate the forgotten army parts cache behind RB-ST on Reserve
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba924: Mark the army parts cache for Tony
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 description:
Prapor's boys are shaving ammo before it reaches the books.
They call it loss, damage, bad counts — soft words for stealing from a man who stopped counting properly. I call it a door. One of those depot rats has a skimmed ammo cache inside K Building, K4, on Reserve. You are not taking it. You are marking it.
My runners will collect later. Prapor's people will keep blaming each other, and the rats will learn there is always a bigger rat with cleaner teeth.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 note: Tony wants you to locate and mark a skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4, on Reserve.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 startedMessageT: Reserve. K Building, K4. Find the skimmed ammo cache and mark it. Do not haul boxes like a mule. My people move the weight.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 successMessageT: Good. The stash is marked. Prapor's rats stole from him, and now I tax the rats. I pulled a few boxes of BP before the rest moved. Do not ask from which pocket.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 failMessageText: You missed the stash or made the wrong kind of noise. Depot rats scatter fast when they smell a bigger crew.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4, marked for his runners.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf2: Locate the skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf3: Mark the skimmed ammo cache for Tony
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 description:
Therapist throws away anything she cannot trust.
That is why she sleeps better than most people in Tarkov, and why men like me still make money. Shoreline has a test batch that never made it back into clean hands: regular injectors, rejected batches, prototype stimulants, old labels, bad storage, maybe a few things Sanitar's people were smart enough not to touch.
The case is in West 301 at the Health Resort. I will give you a key, but it is almost spent, so do not waste trips. Bring the case back sealed. Do not open it. Do not test anything. Demand is ugly, but it pays on time.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 note: Tony wants you to recover an experimental stim case from room W301 at the Health Resort.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 startedMessageT: Shoreline. West wing, room 301. Use the key, recover the stim case, and bring it back sealed. Curiosity is not part of your pay.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 successMessageT: Case is sealed. Labels are ugly. Half of this should probably never touch a living body. That means it will sell. Therapist sorts by safety. I sort by demand.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 failMessageText: You lost the case, opened it, wasted the key, or let Shoreline bury you. None of that moves product.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the experimental stim case recovered from West 301 and brought back sealed.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab07: Locate the experimental stim case in room W301 at the Health Resort
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab0a: Hand over the experimental stim case to Tony
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 description:
Prapor's boys keep losing rifles, ammo, and men in the trees.
I do not waste good boxes on someone who sprays at shadows and dies with a warm barrel. Woods is where old BEARs learned patience. Take a Russian-service weapon, put down five PMCs, and leave alive.
Do that, and I know the better ammo is not being wasted. Access is not a gift. It is a seat closer to the table.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 note: Tony wants you to prove yourself on Woods with Russian-service weapons.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 startedMessageT: Woods. Russian steel. Five PMCs. Walk out with your pulse steady and maybe I open better boxes.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 successMessageT: Red caliber still does honest work in the right hands. I will open a few better boxes for you. Do not mistake access for charity.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 failMessageText: Dead men do not get better ammo. Try again when you can keep your rifle, your pulse, and my investment alive.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 acceptPlayerMes: I'll prove it with Russian steel.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 declinePlayerMe: Not going to Woods for this yet.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 completePlayerM:
Five PMCs down.
I made it out.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd1 changeQuestMess: Tony wants proof you can fight with Russian-service weapons on Woods.
6a3cab9782d8dd83f87b1fd5: Eliminate 5 PMCs on Woods with Russian-service weapons without dying
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 description:
The line into Streets works.
Now I need people on that side who can keep my couriers breathing. There is an old pharmacy on Primorsky Ave, across from LexOs. Front looks dead, but the back room may still be used by a street doctor who treats men who cannot walk into Therapist's place.
Find the clinic, confirm the doctor is still operating, and leave alive. If he is useful, he gets stock. If he gets stock, my crew gets patched. That is how a route becomes a business.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 note: Tony wants you to locate a back-alley clinic contact inside an old pharmacy on Primorsky Ave.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 startedMessageT: Streets. Primorsky Ave, across from LexOs. Find the old pharmacy, check the back room, and do not scare the doctor off.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 successMessageT: The clinic still breathes. The doctor is dirty, quiet, and useful. My couriers get patched, the street owes me favors, and you get field kits through my shelf.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 failMessageText: You missed the clinic or let Streets take your name. A doctor who cannot be found is the same as no doctor at all.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 declinePlayerMe: Not going to Streets for a back-alley doctor yet.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff0 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the old pharmacy clinic on Primorsky Ave checked as a future crew contact.
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff4: Locate the back-alley clinic inside the old pharmacy on Primorsky Ave
6a3fd74f82d8dd83f87b1ff8: Confirm the street doctor is still operating
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 description:
Ragman sells clean fabric and straight stitching.
I need something cheaper. KIBA has seen enough panic, looting, and bad decisions to leave useful scraps behind. First, get inside after the power comes on. Then bring me armor plates and tactical rigs. Torn straps can be sewn. Plates can be re-housed.
Ugly gear is still gear if the right hands touch it. A crew that waits for perfect stock stays poor.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 note: Tony wants you to get into KIBA on Interchange, then bring him armor plates and tactical rigs for cheap refurbished gear.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 startedMessageT: Interchange. Power first, KIBA second. After that, bring me plates and rigs. We build from what other men leave behind.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 successMessageT: Used, burned, cracked, torn — does not matter. The right hands make it usable again, and my people wear armor that cost me less than a favor.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 failMessageText: You came back with nothing useful. KIBA is dangerous, but empty hands do not build shelves.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 acceptPlayerMes: I'll see what KIBA has left.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 declinePlayerMe: Not going into KIBA for salvage yet.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 completePlayerM: Here are the plates and rigs.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b2053 changeQuestMess: Tony wants KIBA checked and salvage materials handed over.
6a413ec882d8dd83f87b205b: Enter KIBA Arms after power has been restored
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 description:
Reserve has rats under the concrete.
Not the hungry kind — the kind with rifles, tools, and boxes of parts they think nobody is counting. They have been stripping military stock and moving repair goods through my lane without paying their cut. I like business. I do not like freeloaders.
Go underground, find the bunker command center, and put down the PMCs working that space. Six bodies should remind the rest that breathing in my lane has a price.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 note: Tony wants you to locate the bunker command center, kill PMCs underground, and extract from Reserve.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 startedMessageT: Reserve. Underground. Find the command center and collect bunker tax from the PMCs working my tunnels.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 successMessageT: The bunker hears Tony now. Parts, tools, repairs — all of it moves cleaner when freeloaders remember there is a price for standing in my lane.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 failMessageText: You found concrete and excuses, not tax. Go back when you can make the bunker listen.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd64946 changeQuestMess: Tony wants PMCs eliminated underground in the Reserve bunker.
6a480d82fb1eff152bd6494a: Locate the bunker command center under Reserve
6a480d82fb1eff152bd6494e: Eliminate 6 PMCs underground in the Reserve bunker
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 description:
The doctor is real.
Good. A doctor without stock is just a man with clean intentions and empty hands. Interchange still has pharmacy stock sitting behind dead counters and locked supply shelves. IDEA side, near the front. There should be a sealed cold-chain case there.
I do not want loose pills, open bottles, or half-rotten garbage. Bring me the sealed case and leave alive. If the cold packs are dead, someone desperate will still call it medicine. Desperation is a market too.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 note: Tony wants you to recover a sealed cold-chain medical case from the IDEA-side pharmacy on Interchange.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 startedMessageT: Interchange. IDEA-side pharmacy, near the front. Find the cold-chain case and get out alive. Seals matter.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 successMessageT: The case is sealed. My street doctor stops working from empty shelves, my couriers stay useful, and the clinic starts owing me properly.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 failMessageText: You lost the case, broke the seals, or let Interchange eat your time. A clinic without stock is just a room with bad lighting.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 declinePlayerMe: Not going to Interchange for clinic stock yet.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64982 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the cold-chain medical case recovered from the IDEA-side pharmacy.
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd64986: Locate the IDEA-side pharmacy on Interchange
6a4920e2fb1eff152bd6498f: Hand over the cold-chain medical case
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 description:
Streets has colors. Not pretty ones — working colors.
Masks, rigs, jackets, little signs men use so their friends know who not to shoot. I want my color remembered. Put on the Death Shadow mask and work the street. Ten PMCs.
Let them see the face before the shooting starts. A symbol is only cloth until people learn to lower their voice around it.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 note: Tony wants you to eliminate PMCs on Streets while wearing a Death Shadow mask.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 startedMessageT: Streets. Death Shadow mask. Ten PMCs. Make the face mean something.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 successMessageT: Good. Now that mask is not just cloth and paint. It is a warning. Ragman understands the value of symbols better than most.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 failMessageText: You wore the face and did not make it matter. Streets does not remember almost.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d2 changeQuestMess: Tony wants PMC kills on Streets while wearing a Death Shadow mask.
6a494a6bfb1eff152bd649d6: Eliminate 10 PMCs on Streets while wearing a Death Shadow mask
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 changeQuestMess: Tony wants close-range PMC kills on Factory with a Desert Eagle.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 declinePlayerMe: Then keep both hands in your pockets.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 failMessageText: The Eagles are loud, but noise is not proof. Go back and make the room understand.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 startedMessageT: Factory. Desert Eagle. Close distance. Put three PMCs down and walk out.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 successMessageT:
Good. Factory heard you. A Desert Eagle is not subtle. It does not whisper, it announces.
You walked into close rooms with that thing and came back alive, so now I know your hands are worth trusting. Take the case. A man doing pocket work needs somewhere clean to keep his tools. I will open the heavy pistol shelf. Do not make me regret it.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 description:
I have work for your hands, not your rifle.
Factory. Close rooms, bad corners, men jumping at shadows. Take these Eagles. Heavy things. Loud things. Not practical, but that is the point. Anybody can hose a hallway with a rifle.
I want to see if you can step into breathing distance with a hand cannon and still put rounds where they belong. Drop PMCs close. Come back alive. Then we talk about the heavy pistol shelf.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba916 note: Kill 3 PMCs on Factory with a Desert Eagle within 20 meters. Survive and extract from Factory. Starts after Quest 15 and should be locked behind Tony LL2.
6a302015d2999bc7b9bba918: Eliminate 3 PMCs on Factory with a Desert Eagle within 20 meters
